methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Kraftblock is a Series B-stage climate and energy technology company founded by Martin Schichtel and Susanne König, having raised €20M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of six same-stage, same-sector companies, supplemented by one public comparable (LNZA) and eight additional private Climate & Energy firms including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc.

The peer-median method forms the primary valuation approach. The six directly comparable Series B companies in Climate & Energy exhibit a median valuation of $355M, with a range spanning $120M to $5.0B. This wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity within the frontier energy subsector—Commonwealth Fusion Systems commands $5.0B at the high end, while valuations cluster between $300M and $500M for peers such as Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M). This peer-median serves as the sole quantitative triangulation method applicable with available data.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $302M to $408M, centered on a $355M median. Key uncertainties include the limited methodological depth—peer median alone cannot fully anchor valuation without revenue multiples, growth rates, or DCF inputs—and the pronounced tail risk represented by Commonwealth Fusion Systems, which materially skews the peer distribution. Further analysis incorporating stage-adjusted revenue or capital efficiency metrics would strengthen conviction in this range.
